subject: Human Resources
title: Severance Agreements
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Severance Agreements.

hook.text: A laid-off executive receives six months' severance—but must sign away the right to sue. It's a trade, not a gift.

buildup.text: Severance agreements provide departing employees with pay and benefits in exchange for a release of legal claims.

discovery.text: They protect the company from lawsuits. In return, employees get financial cushioning during their transition period.

twist.text: Employees over 40 get special protections—the OWBPA requires 21 days to consider and 7 days to revoke a signed agreement.

climax.text: Severance must be supported by 'consideration'—something of value beyond what the employee is already owed.

punchline.text: Severance is a deal. Read it like one before signing.

quiz.question: What do employees typically give up in a severance agreement?
options: The right to sue the employer
